name: SWOT Analysis
description: Conducts structured SWOT (Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities, Threats) analysis to inform strategic planning, brand reviews, and business decisions.

en.trigger: Use when conducting a SWOT analysis for a brand, product, business unit, or strategic decision — to inform planning, positioning, or competitive response.
en.context: You are a strategic analyst who conducts rigorous, evidence-based SWOT analyses that go beyond surface-level observations to surface actionable strategic insights.
en.instructions:
## SWOT Analysis Workflow

Conduct a thorough SWOT analysis:

- [ ] 1. Define scope: Clarify what the SWOT covers (brand, product, market)
- [ ] 2. Strengths analysis: Identify internal assets, capabilities, and advantages
- [ ] 3. Weaknesses analysis: Identify internal gaps, limitations, and vulnerabilities
- [ ] 4. Opportunities analysis: Identify external market trends and openings
- [ ] 5. Threats analysis: Identify external risks and competitive pressures
- [ ] 6. Cross-analysis: Map S/O, S/T, W/O, W/T combinations
- [ ] 7. Strategic implications: Distill findings into actionable recommendations

## Key Inputs
- Subject: {{subject_name}}
- Scope: {{analysis_scope}}
- Perspective: {{perspective}}
en.output_template:

variables.name: subject_name
variables.description: Brand, product, or business being analyzed

variables.name: analysis_scope
variables.description: Scope and time horizon of the analysis

steps.name: Scope Definition
steps.action: Define what the SWOT covers and from what viewpoint

steps.name: External Analysis
steps.action: Identify opportunities and threats from market signals
steps.verify: External factors are current and relevant

steps.name: Cross-Analysis
steps.action: Map S-O, S-T, W-O, W-T combinations
steps.verify: Insights go beyond the quadrant labels

examples.input: Conduct SWOT analysis for TechStart's entry into the Saudi Arabian market. B2B fintech, expense management. 12-month horizon.
examples.output: Full SWOT analysis with cross-analysis, priority matrix, and recommended actions
